mission.title: kyverno: allow updating "generate" rules in ClusterPolicy
mission.description: allow updating "generate" rules in ClusterPolicy. Requested by 9+ users.

steps.title: Check current kyverno deployment
steps.description:
Verify your kyverno version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n kyverno -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kyverno
helm list -n kyverno 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working kyverno installation.

steps.title: Review kyverno configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant kyverno configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n kyverno -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kyverno
kubectl get configmap -n kyverno -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=kyverno
```
As of Kyverno 1.10 many fields in generate rules are now immutable (see PR https://github.com/kyverno/kyverno/pull/6451).

steps.title: Apply the fix for allow updating "generate" rules in ClusterPolicy
steps.description:
> While disabling synchronization is a viable solution to the issue of immutable rules, it presents several challenges:
> 
> * Temporarily turning off synchronization could lead to unforeseen issues in policies that depend on syncing attributes from the source to the generated objects.
```yaml
spec:
          generateExisting: true
          rules:
            - name: sync-certificate-secret
              match:
                any:
                  - resources:
                      kinds:
                        - Namespace
                      namespaces:
                        - one
                        - two
              generate:
                kind: Secret
                apiVersion: v1
                name: certificate
                namespace: "{{request.object.metadata.name}}"
                synchronize: true
                clone:
                  namespace: ku
```

steps.title: Upgrade kyverno to include the fix
steps.description:
If the fix is included in a newer release, upgrade kyverno:
```bash
helm repo update
helm upgrade kyverno kyverno/kyverno --namespace kyverno
```
Verify the upgrade:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n kyverno
helm list -n kyverno
```

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n kyverno -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kyverno
kubectl get events -n kyverno --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "allow updating "generate" rules in ClusterPolicy" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:
> While disabling synchronization is a viable solution to the issue of immutable rules, it presents several challenges:
> 
> * Temporarily turning off synchronization could lead to unforeseen issues in policies that depend on syncing attributes from the source to the generated objects.
codeSnippets:
spec:
          generateExisting: true
          rules:
            - name: sync-certificate-secret
              match:
                any:
                  - resources:
                      kinds:
                        - Namespace
                      namespaces:
                        - one
                        - two
              generate:
                kind: Secret
                apiVersion: v1
                name: certificate
                namespace: "{{request.object.metadata.name}}"
                synchronize: true
                clone:
                  namespace: kube-system
                  name: certificate

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with kyverno installed or the issue environment reproducible.
